patch.value:
## Context

`src/core/fs/fs-utils.ts:29-38`:
```ts
await fs.writeFile(tempPath, contents, 'utf8');
await fs.rename(tempPath, targetPath);
```

On Linux/macOS, `rename(2)` is atomic within a filesystem but raises `EXDEV` across mounts. The same temp-in-same-dir pattern is used for both the LanceDB snapshot (`src/core/search/vector-stores.ts:458-477`) and the drift cache (`src/core/history/drift-scan.ts`). Any deployment where `.agents/pm/` is a bind mount or symlink to a different device will see silent write failures on every mutation.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `writeFileAtomic` catches `EXDEV` and falls back to: write to a tmp in `os.tmpdir()`, copy to target, unlink tmp
- [ ] OR falls back to a non-atomic `writeFile` with a warning so the file is never left corrupt
- [ ] Both `fs-utils.ts` and the LanceDB snapshot writer handle this case
- [ ] Unit test covering the EXDEV code path (mock `rename` to throw EXDEV)

## Evidence

- `src/core/fs/fs-utils.ts:29-38` — no EXDEV handling
- `src/core/search/vector-stores.ts:456-478` — `persistLanceDbLocalTable` also uses writeFile+rename without EXDEV handling
writeFileAtomic cross-device rename safety: handle EXDEV when tmp and target are on different mounts

writeFileAtomic writes a .tmp file in the same directory as the target then renames. If the item store is on a different filesystem mount than the temp-file directory (e.g. symlinked paths, container bind mounts, or network FS), fs.rename raises EXDEV (cross-device link not permitted). There is no fallback to copy+unlink.

- `writeFileAtomic` catches `EXDEV` and falls back to: write to a tmp in `os.tmpdir()`, copy to target, unlink tmp
- OR falls back to a non-atomic `writeFile` with a warning so the file is never left corrupt
- Both `fs-utils.ts` and the LanceDB snapshot writer handle this case
- Unit test covering the EXDEV code path (mock `rename` to throw EXDEV)

message: Start implementation: EXDEV-safe atomic write fallback

value.note: Add EXDEV-safe writeFileAtomic fallback using copy plus cleanup
Use shared atomic writer for LanceDB snapshot persistence
Add EXDEV fallback regression coverage in fs utilities
Add EXDEV fallback coverage for LanceDB snapshot writes

value.author: codex-active-pm-cycle-20260607
value.text: Implemented EXDEV-safe atomic write fallback: src/core/fs/fs-utils.ts now falls back to copyFile+cleanup when rename raises EXDEV; non-EXDEV failures still clean temp then rethrow. LanceDB snapshot persistence now uses writeFileAtomic via src/core/search/vector-stores.ts. Added regressions in tests/unit/fs-utils.spec.ts and tests/unit/vector-store-adapter.spec.ts including EXDEV fallback path. Verification: focused suite pass.

value.author: codex-active-pm-cycle-20260607
value.text: Full verification stack passed (build/typecheck/coverage/contracts/static/security/audit/compatibility/sentry). Linked tests pass via pm test --run for fs-utils + vector-store adapter EXDEV scenarios.
